things.name: Pilsner Urquell Brewery
things.description: Birthplace of the world's first pilsner lager in 1842; guided tours through the historic cellars end with unpasteurised beer direct from wooden barrels

things.name: Republic Square (namesti Republiky)
things.description: One of the largest medieval squares in the Czech Republic, dominated by the Gothic Cathedral of St. Bartholomew and the Renaissance Town Hall

things.name: Cathedral of St. Bartholomew
things.description: Gothic cathedral with the tallest church tower in Bohemia at 102 metres; houses a beautiful 14th-century Madonna of Pilsen statue

things.name: Underground Plzen
things.description: 9km network of medieval cellars and tunnels beneath the old town, used for storage and refuge; guided tours explore this hidden city beneath the streets

things.name: West Bohemian Museum
things.description: Neo-Renaissance museum with extensive natural history, archaeology and art collections representing the region's rich cultural heritage

things.name: Great Synagogue
things.description: Europe's third-largest synagogue built in 1892 in Moorish Revival style; a magnificent landmark and monument to Plzen's former Jewish community

things.name: Patton Memorial Museum
things.description: Museum commemorating the liberation of Plzen by General Patton's US Third Army on 6 May 1945; exhibits of vehicles, weapons and personal items

things.name: Techmania Science Centre
things.description: Interactive science and technology museum in a former Skoda Works building with hands-on exhibits, a planetarium and the Czech Republic's first 3D planetarium

things.name: Doosan Arena
things.description: Home of FC Viktoria Plzen with a 11,700-seat capacity; the club has been a regular Czech champions and UEFA Europa League contender

things.name: Franciscan Monastery and Church
things.description: 13th-century Gothic monastery complex with a beautifully preserved cloister garden and the oldest architectural monument in Plzen
